CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-05-2026 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: No appearance. Notice having been given and there being no appearance or objection filed, the Court orders the case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. Judge: Rochelle East; Clerk: D. Diaz; Not reported. (610)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-20-2025 Continued To Mar-09-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Bryan Wada'S Responses To Request For Production, Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support; And Declaration Of Jason D. Bodie In Support
Off Calendar Jul-29-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1- Defendant FC Company, Inc.'s Motion to Compel from Plaintiff Bryan Wada Responses to Form and Special Interrogatories is GRANTED.

The discovery requests at issue appear reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (See CCP Section 2017.010.) Plaintiff Bryan Wada has not established that he has provided any responses to the form interrogatories. No meet and confer requirement exists for motions to compel responses for Form Interrogatories when no responses have been provided. (CCP Section 2030.290.)

Plaintiff Bryan Wada shall serve verified, objection-free, substantive responses to FC Company, Inc.'s Form Interrogatories, Set One and FC Company, Inc.'s Special Interrogatories, Set One by August 19, 2025.

Plaintiff Bryan Wada did not act with substantial justification in connection with this discovery. Plaintiff Bryan Wada, counsel Huecchi Wong and HH Legal Group, jointly and severally, shall pay $945 to Moving Defendant FC Company, Inc. as sanctions, payment no later than August 19, 2025.

THE PARTIES ARE ADVISED stacked discovery motions are strongly disfavored. Going forward, stacked motions will be ordered off calendar in favor of individual motions focused each on a single discovery vehicle (e.g., Frogs, SRogs, RFPs, RFAs, deposition notice) addressed to a single, specific party.

Order submitted.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff Bryan Wada'S Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One; Special Interrogatories Set One; And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-29-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

2 - Defendant FC Company, Inc.'s Motion to Compel from Plaintiff Bryan Wada Responses to Requests for Production of Documents is GRANTED.

The discovery requests at issue appear reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (See CCP 2017.010.) Plaintiff Bryan Wada has not established that he has provided any responses to the RFPs. No meet and confer requirement exists for motions to compel responses for requests for production of documents when no responses have been provided. (CCP Section 2031.300.)

Plaintiff Bryan Wada shall serve verified, objection-free, responses to FC Company, Inc.'s RFPs, Set One by August 19, 2025.

Plaintiff Bryan Wada did not act with substantial justification in connection with this discovery. Plaintiff Bryan Wada, counsel Huecchi Wong and HH Legal Group, jointly and severally, shall pay $945 to Moving Defendant FC Company, Inc.as sanctions, payment no later than August 19, 2025.

Order submitted.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
